summary: Create minimal M6 Alpha system-complete task graph
acceptance_criteria: Create a minimal, dependency-ordered M6 task DAG under project/tasks/active with explicit owner_role, reviewer_role, risk_level, dependencies, allowed_paths, forbidden_paths, acceptance_criteria, required_tests, route_to, and notes for every M6 task.

The M6 DAG covers Roadmap and acceptance-matrix M6 goals without implementing them: start-to-victory playability, diplomacy, legitimacy, succession completion, layered AI, policy/event/encyclopedia surfaces, three-scenario data pipeline, map candidate path, batch auto-run and balance dashboard, accessibility core flows, Alpha no P0/P1 validation, and adviser intervention evidence.

The M6 DAG explicitly preserves the M5 product-owner decision DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE: no manual node battle implementation task, no manual node battle blocking spike, and no manual node battle research task may block M6, M7, or M8.

The M6 DAG preserves Human Gates for frozen product/platform/core architecture changes, paid services, accounts/secrets, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial decisions, branding, major cultural-risk decisions, irreversible migrations, and any formal 1.0 scope reversal.
Tasks are small enough for independent writer/reviewer routing, with no overlapping writer ownership for the same package or file region.

No product code, package code, content-source data, roadmap edits, accepted ADR changes, M6 implementation work, production dependency changes, release work, telemetry, networking, or mod support is performed by this planning task.
systems_architect writer and independent qa_reviewer reviewer produce validated handoffs before lead acceptance.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for all new M6 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape
notes: M6 starts only after M5 Gate PASS_WITH_LIMITS entered origin/main and M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 is CLOSED.

This task creates or restores the M6 task graph only; it must not implement diplomacy, legitimacy, layered AI, policy/events, encyclopedia, scenarios, map candidates, balance dashboard, accessibility, or Alpha validation behavior.
Manual node battle remains deferred from M5 and the 1.0 mainline; do not create an implementation task or blocking spike/research task for M6, M7, or M8.
M6 may create tasks that document the already-made manual node battle deferral only as non-blocking post-1.0 candidate tracking, not implementation authorization.
M5 Gate PASS_WITH_LIMITS carries forward bounded limits from docs/GATE-M5.md and must not be re-described as an unconditional PASS.
Created a dependency-ordered M6 DAG with the first executable downstream task set to READY and all other downstream M6 tasks left in DRAFT for lead selection after QA review.
The M6 DAG contains no manual node battle implementation task and no blocking manual node battle spike or research task for M6, M7, or M8.
